article_title: Historical evolution of microvascular decompression after Jannetta’s establishment: Anatomical maps and physiological compasses—a narrative review
label: Fig. 4
caption: Lateral supracerebellar-infratentorial route for TN. Tailored exposure along the tentorial surface provides a direct view of the trigeminal nerve while minimizing cerebellar retraction and avoiding manipulation of the superior petrosal vein. Modified with permission from Matsushima et al. [40]

article_title: Analysis of Pontine cavernous malformation resection based on 3D microanatomical study
label: Fig. 7
caption: A-D Preoperative CT and MRI demonstrate right-sided multiple PCMs, with hemorrhagic stroke in the dorsolateral pontine CMs. E-F Utilizing a right extreme lateral supracerebellar infratentorial approach, intraoperative neurophysiological mapping excluded eloquent areas. The IPS was identified using the trochlear nerve as its superior boundary marker, followed by entry into the brainstem via the IPS. G The lesion was resected while preserving the associated draining veins. H-J Postoperative imaging confirms complete resection of the hemorrhagic dorsolateral PCMs. K Histopathological examination confirmed CMs

article_title: Comparison of surgical approaches and outcome for symptomatic pineal cysts: microscopic/endoscopic fenestration vs. stereotactic catheter implantation
label: Fig. 3
caption: Pre (a-c) and postoperative (d-f) MRI imaging of a hemorrhaged pineal cyst (white *) and consecutive aqueduct stenosis with hydrocephalus occlusus removed by microscopic approach via a supracerebellar infrantentorial craniotomy

article_title: Subarachnoid cisterns as surgical corridors: Integrating microsurgical anatomy and neuroimaging for intracranial navigation
label: Figure 14:
caption: (A) Basal view of the cerebrum. The posterior cerebellomedullary cistern lies dorsal to the bulb and cerebellar vermis and is divided by the PICA membranes into one medial compartment and two lateral compartments, which are not well appreciated in this specimen. The cortical segment of the PICA travels through this cistern dividing into medial and lateral trunks after passing through the telovelotonsillar fissure (green arrows). (B) Magnetic resonance T2 sagittal plane showing the infratentorial cisterns with the vertebral artery (red arrow) passing through the premedullary cistern and ascending to the prepontine cistern to merge with its homonymous contralateral to form the basilar artery (blue arrow). Hypointense images in relation to the CSF can be seen in the posterior cerebellomedullary cistern (green arrow) which may correspond to the VA and PICA. The apex of the fourth ventricle, fastigium (yellow arrow), is usually related to the cranial loop of the PICA (inset: sagittal plane showing the highlighted area). VA: Vertebral artery, PICA: Posterior inferior cerebellar artery, CSF: Cerebrospinal fluid.

article_title: Endoscope-Assisted Extreme Lateral Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach for Resection of Superior Cerebellar Peduncle Pilocytic Astrocytoma: Technical Note
label: Figure 1
caption: (A) T1-weighted MRI sagittal image showing a mass centered on superior cerebellar peduncle, spontaneously iso-intense. (B) Contrast enhanced T1 sagittal MRI showing peripheral enhancement. (C) T2 axial MRI revealing a heterogeneous signal intensity suggesting acute intralesional hemorrhage and obstructive hydrocephalus.

article_title: Endoscope-Assisted Extreme Lateral Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach for Resection of Superior Cerebellar Peduncle Pilocytic Astrocytoma: Technical Note
label: Figure 2
caption: (A) Post-ETV CISS MRI showing patent stoma and regression of hydrocephalus. (B) Coronal T1-weighted MRI showing a well localized mass on the SCP.

article_title: Endoscope-Assisted Extreme Lateral Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach for Resection of Superior Cerebellar Peduncle Pilocytic Astrocytoma: Technical Note
label: Figure 3
caption: Artist work showing the appropriate head positioning. The head is slightly flexed and turned 45° towards the floor in order to place the genu of the sigmoid sinus at the highest point of a perpendicular line that bisects the sinodural angle.

article_title: Endoscope-Assisted Extreme Lateral Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach for Resection of Superior Cerebellar Peduncle Pilocytic Astrocytoma: Technical Note
label: Figure 4
caption: T2-weighted MRI showing the following:(1:)culmen; (2) central lobule; (3) lingula; black arrow: the preculminate sulcus between the culmen and central lobule; yellow arrow: precentral cerebellar sulcus between the central lobule and the lingula.

article_title: Endoscope-Assisted Extreme Lateral Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach for Resection of Superior Cerebellar Peduncle Pilocytic Astrocytoma: Technical Note
label: Figure 5
caption: Immediate post-operative MRI (A) sagittal contrast enhanced, (B) T2 sagittal and (C) T2 axial showing near complete resection with a residual tumor on the latero-inferior surface of the tectum (arrow).

article_title: Endoscope-Assisted Extreme Lateral Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach for Resection of Superior Cerebellar Peduncle Pilocytic Astrocytoma: Technical Note
label: Figure 6
caption: Histological analysis showed a moderately cellular glial neoplasm with biphasic growth pattern (A), characterized by tumor cells with bipolar processes and the presence of occasional eosinophilic granular bodies (B).
